title: 怪屋
summary:
Sophia informs Charles that the house is cold because the doors have been closed. She provides a timeline of the morning's activities: Eustace and Josephine were doing homework until 12:30 PM, with a break at 10:30 AM, while Philip was likely in the library. Sophia's mother emerged from the bathroom around 12:15 PM. In the library, Charles finds Philip looking exhausted and Margaret crying; Margaret expresses intense guilt, calling Josephine her "little monster" and fearing the child will die.

Charles finds the nurse crying in the kitchen. She claims an evil aura exists in the house and believes someone killed the master and attempted to kill Josephine. The nurse notes that Josephine was an eavesdropper who used secrets to threaten others, though she suggests such tactics would not work against a poisoner. When Charles asks about Josephine's black leather notebook, the nurse does not know its location.

Taverner calls Charles to Josephine's room, which has been ransacked; the drawers are pulled out, sheets are torn, and photos have been removed from frames. Taverner notes that anyone in the house—including Philip, Margaret, the nanny, Brenda, Lawrence, Eustace, Miss de Harvilland, or Roger—could have committed the act, as their alibis are insufficient. Recalling that Josephine once mentioned "scouting" in the attic, Charles searches the water tank room. He discovers a stack of letters wrapped in brown paper hidden behind a large water tank. The letters are from Brenda Lionides to Lawrence Brown, expressing a secret, intense love and suggesting a plan to be together. Taverner concludes that Brenda and Lawrence have been planning something for some time.
